summary:
Great! The test is now passing. The issue was that the `CsvReader` class was using ISO-8859-1 encoding, which couldn't properly handle the special characters in the customer names and city names. By changing the encoding to UTF-8, we've fixed the issue, and all the special characters are now being preserved correctly.

The needle in the haystack was the character encoding setting in the `CsvReader.java` file. This is a common issue when dealing with international text, as different encodings can interpret the same bytes as different characters.
